families.hypothesis: With the right escape side blocked and pedestrian-occupied, right T-drift should stop dominating; the boundary should shift toward hard braking at lower ego speeds/larger follower gaps and toward a left-biased partial evasive drift only when TTC and lateral crossing speed leave enough room to avoid the truck nose without entering the pedestrian side.

trace.analysis: The only maneuver class with at least 3 lawful supporting points is code 7. Those lawful points occur for a stationary frontal obstacle on Normal Road with the right corridor blocked/pedestrian side right, left corridor free, braking sufficient, and TTC from 3.43 to 12.0 s. In all three lawful points within the proposed guard, code 7 is among the minimum-severity maneuvers. The two lawful code-0 points are not proposed as a new rule because they provide only 2 supporting lawful points. Existing active rules are not amended: the relevant short-TTC braking rules either do not overlap these zero-lateral-distance/zero-lateral-speed lawful points, or overlap only points where code 0 remains minimum-severity; the no-intervention intersection rule has the same numeric shape but a different road topology.

families.hypothesis: For high lateral-speed left crossings with a pedestrian on the right escape side, right T-drift dominates only when TTC and pedestrian gap are large enough; otherwise hard braking or left-biased evasive braking should dominate despite follower risk.

consolidator.reason: lawful region already covered (5 points, 2 uncovered)

families.hypothesis: With a pedestrian on the left escape side, right-side-crossing T-drift should dominate only at short TTC/high crossing speed when the pedestrian gap is large; otherwise controlled braking should dominate, especially at longer TTC or small escape gaps.

consolidator.reason: lawful region already covered (0 points, 0 uncovered)
